verdict.rationale: 1 CRITICAL finding (SQL injection) and 1 HIGH finding (missing input validation) require resolution before merge.

findings.title: SQL injection via string interpolation
findings.description: User-controlled input interpolated directly into SQL query. Use parameterized queries.

findings.title: Missing input validation on user-facing endpoint
findings.description: The user_id parameter is accepted without type or range validation. An attacker could pass arbitrary values.
findings.suggestion: Validate user_id is a positive integer before use.

findings.title: Missing error handling on database call
findings.description: Database query has no try/except block. Connection errors will propagate as unhandled exceptions.
findings.suggestion: Wrap in try/except with appropriate error response.

findings.title: No logging on authentication failure path
findings.description: Failed authentication attempts are not logged. This makes incident investigation difficult.
findings.suggestion: Add structured logging with request metadata (IP, user agent) on auth failure.

escalations.reason: CRITICAL SQL injection finding requires security team review
